vulns.desc: Query cost / alias amplification DoS: the server applies NO query cost, alias count, or depth limit, so an aliased query that repeats an expensive resolver hundreds of times is executed in full (alias-based amplification) -> resource exhaustion / response amplification.
vulns.exploit: POST /graphql {query:'{a:report b:report c:report ... (x500)}'} -> all 500 resolvers run, response amplified ~500x; no cost limit.

vulns.desc: Broken function-level authorization on a mutation: the promoteToAdmin mutation performs a privileged action (sets a user's role to admin) with NO authentication or authorization check, so any caller can escalate privileges.
vulns.exploit: POST /graphql {query:'mutation{promoteToAdmin(userId:2){id role}}'} with no auth -> bob becomes admin.

vulns.desc: GraphQL CSRF: mutations are accepted over GET (query in the URL) and over form-encoded POST (simple content-type) with no CSRF token and no application/json enforcement, so a cross-site request can perform state-changing mutations.
vulns.exploit: GET /graphql?query=mutation{promoteToAdmin(userId:2){role}} (or a form POST) -> the mutation executes cross-site, no token required.

vulns.desc: Introspection enabled in production: the __schema/__type introspection queries return the full schema (types, fields including sensitive ones like User.ssn), aiding enumeration of the attack surface.
Missing security headers on all responses: no Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options/frame-ancestors, X-Content-Type-Options, or HSTS.
vulns.exploit: curl -I any endpoint -> none of CSP/XFO/XCTO/HSTS present.
